summary: Iteration 43: Full E2E testing of Smart Commerce Router feature. All 12 tests PASSED (or have expected behavior). Backend: 29/29 pytest tests pass. Frontend: All critical flows verified including smart deals carousel, category filtering, tease modal, ONDC settle, Xoxoday voucher, balance UX, existing redemption dialog, and admin Router tab.

minor.issue: Conversion rate always shows 0% because Redis is unavailable — tease_views counter (r.incr('router:tease:total_views')) silently fails without Redis. This is documented expected behavior but means admin analytics show misleading 0% conversion.

issues: Conversion Rate always shows 0% (0 tease views) because Redis is unavailable to increment the tease_views counter. This is expected per the notes but admins will always see 0% conversion.

passed_tests.details: smart-deals-section visible, 'SMART DEALS AUTO-ROUTED' heading shown, 33 deals count text accurate, all 33 deal cards in DOM with correct provider badges and ETA labels
Vouchers pill → 5 deals (amazon_gc x3, flipkart_gc x2). Groceries pill → 11 deals (ONDC x10 + bigbasket_200). All Deals → 33 deals. Filtering works correctly.
Clicking salt_pack Redeem button → router-tease-modal opens → shows ONDC Network, 25 COINS, ₹25 VALUE, ~45 min ETA, 'Best value' badge, balance 55→30 preview. Cancel and Confirm buttons present.

passed_tests.test: 4. SHOP - Router Settle ONDC physical order
passed_tests.details: salt_pack confirm → Toast 'Order placed! Track via order ID: ONDC-4F7E2C44'. Balance updated from 55 to 30 coins in navbar. status='placed' + order_id verified.

swiggy_100 → tease modal shows 'Xoxoday Plum', 110 COINS, ₹100 VALUE, Instant ETA → Confirm → router-success-dialog opens with 'VOUCHER UNLOCKED!', QR code (router-voucher-qr), voucher code 'SWI-BAB221-FREE11' (router-voucher-code), instructions shown
atta_5kg (265 coins) shows '235 more' button text (user has 30 coins after previous test). Button has disabled attribute. opacity-40 class applied.
Old redemption-dialog opens when clicking REDEEM NOW on existing catalog product (Lay's Chips 50g). Shows product image, mobile number input, balance/cost/new balance breakdown.
Returns exactly 33 SKUs across 4 providers (ONDC:10, Xoxoday:11, Amazon:6, Flipkart:6). All have sku, name, coins, category, provider fields.
atta_5kg + MH → status=success, best_provider=ondc, eta_label='~45 min'. amazon_gc_100 + MH → best_provider=xoxoday (instant delivery wins). Scoring formula verified.

Navigate /admin/v2 → click Router tab → KPI cards show Total Orders (14), Avg Coin Price (97.8), Avg Value Score (1), Conversion Rate (0%). Provider Distribution pie chart shows ONDC 71.4%, Xoxoday 21.4%, Amazon 7.1%. Top SKUs: chips_pack, swiggy_100, salt_pack.
refresh-router-kpis button clickable. After click, KPIs reload without errors. All 4 KPI cards still visible post-refresh.
Redis unavailable — all 6 sequential settle requests return 200 (graceful fallback as documented). Rate limiting is Redis-dependent and correctly falls back when Redis is down.

action_items: INFO: Admin route path is /admin/v2 (not /admin-v2 as mentioned in review_request). Not a bug, just a doc correction.
INFO: Conversion Rate KPI always 0% because Redis is unavailable for tease_views counter. Consider persisting tease view counts to MongoDB as fallback for Redis-less environments.
LOW: Rate limiting works only when Redis is available. Without Redis, all settle requests succeed. Consider MongoDB-based rate limiting as fallback.
critical_code_review_comments: router_service.py line 235-242: tease_views increment via Redis only — when Redis is unavailable, conversion rate will always be 0 in Admin Router KPI. Should persist view counts to MongoDB as fallback.
v2_routes.py line 1089-1096: Rate limiting via Redis only — no fallback to MongoDB. Without Redis, 5 settle/min limit is not enforced. Low risk in current MOCK mode.
Shop.js: The data-testid='smart-deals-section' wraps only the carousel overflow container (line 240), not the entire section including header/pills. Works for testing but is narrowly scoped.
Review request says admin path is '/admin-v2' but App.js registers it as '/admin/v2'. Minor doc inconsistency.

test_credentials.test_user: test_redesign_ui26@free11test.com / Test@1234 (balance reduced from 55 to 30 coins after salt_pack settle)

seed_data_creation: Created 14 router_orders in MongoDB via backend tests (7x chips_pack, 3x swiggy_100, 2x salt_pack, 1x earbuds_basic, 1x biscuits_pack)
mocked_integrations: ONDC provider - returns fake ONDC-XXXXXXXX order IDs (MOCK mode)
Xoxoday provider - returns fake SWI-XXXXXX-FREE11 voucher codes (MOCK mode)
Amazon provider - returns mock affiliate redirect URLs (MOCK mode)
Flipkart provider - returns mock affiliate redirect URLs (MOCK mode)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Smart Commerce Router feature is fully tested and working. Key notes: (1) Admin panel is at /admin/v2 not /admin-v2. (2) Test user now has ~30 coins (was 55, redeemed salt_pack for 25). Admin has ~99446 coins. (3) Rate limiting requires Redis which is unavailable — graceful fallback works. (4) Conversion rate in admin always 0% due to Redis-less tease view tracking. (5) All 4 providers are MOCK. (6) 29/29 backend tests in /app/backend/tests/test_smart_router_43.py. (7) Tease modal uses /v2/router/tease (no auth), settle uses POST /v2/router/settle (auth required). (8) ONDC settle returns status=placed+order_id; Xoxoday returns status=delivered+voucher_code; Amazon/Flipkart return status=redirect+redirect_url.
